laws.title: Public Law 9-59 | M.P.D Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology keeps PL-009-059 in repeal history only; it is not live Code text and should remain a concise historical record.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Retain only a concise repeal-history note for the M.P.D. Act and remove the long dump-style note content and Trello link.

audit/primary/batch-11-03.json classifies PL-009-059 as fully-repealed with historical-note-only treatment.
usc/usc40.xml keeps the branch note at the Title 40 anchor, while origin/main does not carry the same cleaned-up branch note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch retains a repeal-history note in usc/usc40.xml at the Title 40 anchor, but it is overlong and still needs cleanup; origin/main does not show the same branch note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep only a concise repeal-history note and strip the dump-style retention and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 9-63 | The Federal District Employment Act of 2022

laws.title: Public Law 10-65 | Federal Judicial Reform Act ii
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology treats PL-010-065 as a historical court-opinion record rather than enacted statutory text, so it remains history only.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Do not codify the court opinion as positive law; keep only a short historical cross-reference if the record must be preserved at all.
source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-010-065/law.txt identifies the source as a court opinion rather than enacted statutory text.
audit/primary/batch-12-01.json classifies PL-010-065 as fully-repealed / incorrectly implemented with historical-note-only treatment.
usc/usc50.xml carries the branch history-note treatment, while origin/main does not supply a better statutory anchor.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch treats the opinion as a Title 50 note in usc/usc50.xml, but that does not make it enacted law; origin/main does not supply a proper positive-law codification.
laws.final_recommended_action: Remove the dump-style retention and keep only a short historical cross-reference.

laws.title: Public Law 11-70 | Supreme Court Please Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology keeps PL-011-070 current, but the transfer instruction must be placed as a real transfer note at the correct 40 U.S.C. anchor rather than left in a note-only Title 28 wrapper.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Carry the Section 2 transfer instruction to the 40 U.S.C. 6121 anchor, keep the short title and effective date as notes, and remove the dump-style wrapper.

audit/primary/batch-12-01.json classifies the transfer instruction as a transfer-note that points to 40 U.S.C. 6121.
usc/usc28.xml shows the current branch still parked at a Title 28 note anchor, while origin/main does not show the same branch note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch leaves PL-011-070 in a note-only block at Title 28, but the transfer instruction still belongs at 40 U.S.C. 6121; origin/main does not show the same branch note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Move the transfer note to 40 U.S.C. 6121 and keep the remaining material as concise note history.

laws.final_recommended_action: Apply the substantive 28 U.S.C. 1 amendment and remove the note-only substitute.

laws.title: Public Law 12-74 | Federal Bar Association Act of 2023
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology keeps PL-012-074 current, with sections 4 and 5 requiring positive-law Title 5 placement while the PL-007-047 repeal remains history only.
laws.exact_enacted_text_or_amendment_command: Codify sections 4 and 5 as new Title 5 provisions at the proper Title 5 anchor, and keep the repeal of PL-007-047 as history only.

usc/usc05.xml shows the branch note in Title 5, while origin/main does not yet show the needed positive-law Title 5 sections.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch parks PL-012-074 as a note-only block in usc/usc05.xml, but the operative sections still need to be codified in Title 5; origin/main does not show the needed Title 5 implementation.
